title: centaurea triumfettii subsp. adscendens
intro:
Centaurea triumfettii subsp. adscendens is a plant species that belongs to the family Asteraceae, commonly known as the Kermes oak or kermes oak centaury, characterized by its small, white or pink flowers and edible seeds.

This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antifungal properties. The health benefits of Centaurea triumfettii subsp. adscendens include its potential to treat digestive issues, skin problems, and respiratory infections. The herbal preparations of this plant include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which can be used to make teas, salves, and topical creams for various health conditions.
